mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances or t-statistic for mean return difference is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to yield a positive return frequency above 55% over 60-day horizons in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which increases corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative 20-day forward returns in >60% of observations during late-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls indicates sustained labor market strength, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window or positive return frequency falls below 50% when payrolls rise.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price index growth exceeding 2.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of instances when inflation crosses the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarter-over-quarter expansion in real GDP and month-over-month growth in retail sales validates robust aggregate demand, reinforcing earnings forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a negative return frequency above 55% over 20-day horizons in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and impending earnings downgrades, triggering immediate sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum produces positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of observations during post-shock recovery phases.
